questions.question: What is the purpose of a constructor in Java?
options: A) To create an object
B) To assign values to instance variables
C) To call other methods
D) To declare a class
questions.explanation: A constructor is used to initialize an object when it is created, assigning values to instance variables.

questions.question: What does the 'this' keyword refer to in Java?
options: A) The current object
B) The class of the current object
C) The parent class
D) The superclass
questions.explanation: The 'this' keyword is used to refer to the current object, allowing you to access its members and variables.

questions.question: What happens to objects when they are no longer needed in Java?
options: A) They are immediately deleted
B) They are placed in memory for future use
C) They are reutilized by the garbage collector
D) They are ignored by the system
questions.explanation: Objects are placed in memory for future use by the garbage collector until they are no longer needed, at which point they are deleted.

questions.question: When is garbage collection performed in Java?
options: A) When a new object is created
B) When an object is no longer needed
C) When the program is terminated
D) At compile time
questions.explanation: Garbage collection is performed by the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) to free up memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use.

questions.question: What is a method in Java?
options: A) A block of code that performs a specific task
B) A variable that stores a value
C) A class that defines a blueprint for objects
D) An object that represents a single entity
questions.explanation: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task, allowing you to reuse code and organize your program more effectively.
